Kawabata Makoto.title: えろちか 雷舞1991 Erochika Live 1991
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Erochika (1984 - 1992) is the X-certificate Japanese-style prog band that was led by Makoto Kawabata.
An erotic world explosion of rhythm changes, pentatonic scales, and archaic lyrics!
The original cassette book was self-released before split up in December 1991.
This digital album was remastered for Bandcamp by Kawabata Makoto.

Kawabata Makoto.title: 1st Trip (ファースト・トリップ）
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1980, then re-issued on a 10 CDR box set entitled “Makoto Kawabata early works 1978-1981” in 2000. (Afterward, this 10 CDR box was changed to the 11 CDR + 1CD box.)
It was reissued on vinyl by Italian QBICO Records in 2002.
"Our first release after changing the name of the band from the Dark Revolutionary Collective to Baroque Bordello. With our concept of restoring the independence that music possesses in and of itself, we began composing and playing in an improvised idiom that refused to be tied to any pre-existing style. As the synthesizer was still the only instrument that we owned, we were forced to build the other instruments ourselves. The A-side of the tape was made up of performances on these homemade instruments. The B-side was an electronic soundtrack for aerobics, incorporating some doubtless Pink Floyd-inspired sound effects. There was also a piece for guitar and cable noise. Both sides sounded very weird” – Makoto Kawabata (2002)
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released September 17, 2020
1. I’m God (全能なる創造者)
2. Die A Martyr (殉教者たれ)
3. Branded As Fool (愚者の烙印)
4. Envoy From The Darkness (暗闇の使者)
5. P-C2TG52-3KR32 (愚女に捧げる〜SONIC BOOMの瞬間的ノルティマスパズル的分解によって生じるニーダから構成されるシンセサイザーのための暗号「P-C2TG52-3KR32」)
6. Electric Shock (エレクトリック・ショック)
Baroque Bordello :
Kawabata Makoto : pygmy guitar, can bass, harpshtube, keyboard, voice
Kawagishi Tetsushi : pygmy guitar, can bass, harpshtube, percussion, voice, electric guitar on #6
Iwaki Yasuo : percussion, harpshtube, synthesizer, voice
Recorded at R.E.P. studio and Horyuji Sound House, 1979
Produced & engineered by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: 4 Tunes
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1981, then re-issued on a 10 CDR box set entitled “Makoto Kawabata early works 1978-1981” in 2000. (Afterward, this 10 CDR box was changed to the 11 CDR + 1CD box.)
As I played with my band Baroque Bordello, i also kept on experimenting with solo recordings. As the group broke new ground as "awakened pop", my solo work proceeded by a continual process of trial and error towards the unaffordable and undeniable idea of an "absolute music".

Kawabata Makoto.title: A Soul That Eats Flesh and Melts : Live in Kobe 2025
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The latest live recording of Mainliner from Japan Tour 2025.
Unable to stop the songs from getting longer, Mainliner finally played three songs in a 90-minute set.
The riffs seemed to repeat themselves forever, and the guitar solos were explosive. A milestone in minimal trance psychedelic.
This digital live album was released exclusively on Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Abnormal Songs vol.1 アブノーマル・ソングス vol.1
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This cassette contained music that Kawabata composed for a play he had written called “Alice in Nakedland”.
The play was some erotic expressionist nonsense - sort of like “Alice in Wonderland” and “The Cabinet of Dr. Caligari” added together then divided by two. His script was used, but his music was rejected by the other stuff members.
The original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1980. then re-issued on a 10 CDR box set entitled “Makoto Kawabata early works 1978-1981” in 2000. (Afterward, this 10 CDR box was changed to the 11 CDR + 1CD box.) It was reissued on vinyl by Italian QBICO Records in 2003.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Acid Balloon Cosmic Drive Earth Frontier
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This original cassette “Acid Balloon Cosmic Drived Earth”, in a limited edition of 20, was sold at gigs during Musica Transonic’s 1996 Europe tour. It is a musique concrete soundscape recorded in Paris with Yoshida Tatsuya (Ruins), YOKO (ex Super Ball) and Coras. The first ten copies have an entirely different mix to the second ten.
Kawabata overdubbed and remixed as his solo work “Acid Balloon Cosmic Drive Earth Frontier” after the tour though, it has been unreleased.
This digital album was released only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: AFRIMAKOTO
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is a special new digital album of the collaborated trio AFRIMAKOTO by Osaka based wild primitive girls duo Afrirampo and Kawabata Makoto of Acid Mothers Temple.
This is the latest studio recording session on 14th September 2020.
All music and words were totally improvised, also No over-dubbed No edited.
Also Kawabata Makoto joined Afrirampo's streaming live on 7th October 2020.
This album was released on Bandcamp first, and this has been reissued on limited CD by Acid Mothers Temple label in 2021.
[Afrirampo]
Formed in Osaka in 2002. Guitar rock duo consisting of Oni (vo, g) and Pika (vo, dr). The primitive musicality of their sound and their astounding live shows quickly brought them national and international notoriety.
Released their debut album "A" in 2003. In 2004 they went to live and record with a Baka pygmy community deep in the forests of Cameroon, with a CD of these recordings being released in 2006 as "Baka ga kita!!" In 2005 they released "Urusa in Japan" on the Japanese major label, Ki/oon Records, and "Koregamayakuda", their first overseas release on Tzadik. Toured the US, and Europe with Sonic Youth.
Released a collaborative album with Acid Mothers Temple called "We're Acid Mothers Afrirampo!" and played shows together.
Played at the All Tomorrow's Parties festival, where they performed together with Yoko Ono.
Released "Suuto Breakor" in 2007 and "We are Uchu no Ko" in 2010.
In 2010 they announced that they were breaking up and in 2001 they released a CD and DVD called "Never Ending Afrirampo" (Ki/oon Records) of their farewell show.
Six years later, in 2016, they reformed. Released "Afriverse" in 2018.
Both members are now mothers in their private lives, but they continue to perform at a more relaxed mommy's pace.
afrirampo.net

Kawabata Makoto.title: Andromelos
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Andromelos were formed back in 2004 by Yamazaki Maso (electronics) aka.Masonna, Kawabata Makoto (guitar, organ), and Okano Futoshi (drums). They released their first self-titled album on the Finnish label Ektro Records in 2006. But since then, for all kinds of tedious reasons, the band have been unable to play even a single gig. But they were back as Andromelos2 in 2013, this time as the trio of Kawabata, Okano and Nakaya Koichi (electronics) from Nasca Car! They will release a new album "Electric Reincarnation" from the French label BamBalam Records in this November.
The original CD was released by Ektro Records in 2006.
This digital album was remastered and remade the artworks by Kawabata Makoto for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai 暗黒革命共同体 (Dark Revolutionary Collective)
Kawabata Makoto.artist: Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai 暗黒革命共同体 (Dark Revolutionary Collective)
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai is Kawabata's first group that was formed in 1978. This is Kawabata's first studio recordings in his carrier. Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai is just an initial impulse even there wasn't instruments like guitar, bass or drums. The original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1980, then re-issued on a 10 CDR box set entitled “Makoto Kawabata early works 1978-1981” in 2000. (Afterward, this 10 CDR box was changed to the 11 CDR + 1CD box.). This digital album was remastered by Kawabata Makoto only for Bandcamp.
The only studio recording by the first group Kawabata Makoto formed with two friends. Since Kawabata was at primary school he had loved Indian music, musique concrete, and the electronic music of Stockhausen etc. Then when he was 12 he discovered The Who, Deep Purple and Led Zeppelin… if only there was something that combined hard rock with electronic music, he would dream to himself. That was the beginning.
At the time they didn’t own any instruments. The only thing they had was a synthesizer that they had borrowed from another friend. As a result, their first musical forays were played on that one synthesizer and some handmade percussion.
They laid down the basic tracks in the dark storeroom adjoining the science laboratory at their junior high school, under the light from a single red lightbulb. They used some of the beakers and instruments from the storeroom as their percussion, breaking several of them in the process. Needless to say, this episode got them into a heap of trouble later.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released April 28, 2022
1. 暗黒革命 Dark Revolution
2. 狂人同盟 Madmen Union
Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai :
Kawabata Makoto : percussion, syunthesizer
Kawagishi Tetsushi : percussion
Iwaki Yasuo : percussion
music by Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai
recorded at Dark Room, 1978

Kawabata Makoto.title: Aurora Ecstasy From Planet 33
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is an unreleased rare live recording of a space jazz rock band GODMAN in 2008.
GODMAN was formed in 2005 by 2 members from Acid Mothers Temple (Kawabata Makoto and Higashi Hiroshi) and the Tokyo based psychedelic jam band Mandog (Miyashita Keiichi, Enomoto Takayuki, and Watanabe Yasuyuki) and released their first album "GOD ∞ DOG" on the Acid Mothers Temple Label.
The group had played shows irregularly, they've changed line-up for each show.
This line-up is 3 founder members (Miyashita, Kawabata and Higashi) and Tabata, also futures 2 young musicians Sakaguchi Mitsuhisa and Ichiraku Yoshiyuki.
Sakaguchi Mitsuhisa had played with Kawabata as a member of Mothers of Invasion several times.
Ichiraku Yoshiyuki had played with Kawabata as a member of Nishinihon, too.
The original sources are 2 kinds of audience recordings, then Kawabata mixed both.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released September 8, 2021
1. Aurora Ecstasy From Planet 33 ~ Super Gold ~ Plutonian Plutonium ~ Overlord Obliteration
GODMAN :
Miyashita Keiichi : guitar, voice (from Mandog)
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
Higashi Hiroshi : synthesizer
Tabata Mitsuru : bass
Ichiraku Yoshiyuki : drums (from Guruhost, ex.FRATENN)
Sakaguchi Mitsuhisa : keyboards (from Guruhost, ex.Haizara)
All Music is totally improvised, no edited and no over-dubbed
Recorded live at Club Goodman (Tokyo) 18th December 2008
Produced and mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Basement Echo
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Basement Echo is the historical meeting point of two generations of the Japanese underground. Kawabata Makoto, leader of The Acid Mothers Temple, and Michishita Shinsuke, leader of LSD March, are together here for the first time in a guitar duo. Though Kawabata and Michishita are from the same scene, the fact that they are from different generation means that they bring different energy to the collaborations. This fact is extremely exciting for Kawabata, who refers to Michishita as "one of the most promising musicians in the Japanese Underground."
Both artists agree that Basement Echo was a step into the unknown with neither really knowing what the resulting sounds would be.
For Michishita the collaborations have been a dream come true. He describes Kawabata as having a "soul of rock that never burns out." After the recordings were complete the two friends sat down and raised a triumphant toast. Recorded at Snowflake Sound in Sapporo in October of 2007. Totally improvised with no editing/overdubbing. Designed by Darryl Norsen.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released November 30, 2023
1. Good Morning Dragon
2. Sister
3. South Sun
4. Yellow Mellow Love
5. Pink New York
Kawabata Makoto : electric guitar
Michishita Shinsuke : electric guitar
All music was improvised, no overdubbed
Recorded at Snowflake Sound (Sapporo) 21st October 2007
Engineered by Richard Horner
Art works by Darryl Norsen

Kawabata Makoto.title: Birth of Nishinihon 西日本爆誕
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is an unreleased live recording of the dawn of Nishinihon’s. (Probably this is the memorable debut live!)
Nishinihon was formed by Tsuyama Atsushi, Ichiraku Yoshimitsu and Kawabata Makoto as "Old-Fashioned Art Rock Band in the end of this millennium" in 1999.
(Tsuyama was the bassist of Acid Mothers Temple & The Melting Paraiso U.F.O. in 1999 - 2015, also Ichiraku was the drummer in 2001.)
They released an album “If You Smell What Art Rock is Cookin! (アートロックの新鋭)” on CD from Gyuune Cassette in 2000, and on dbl LP from Static Caravan / Resonant reissued with a bonus track in 2002.
Track-2 and Track-3 are early versions of songs are included in 1st album, and these songs' names are different from studio versions.
Kawabata broke a guitar string on Track-1, that’s why no guitar on the middle of the song.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released April 14, 2022
1. Theme of Nishinihon
2. JR Nishinihon (aka.Hoos The Fuk That Et Tha Rest O Man Schoo?)
3. Dottoko (aka. Constitution Of The State Of Western Japan, Article 14)
4. No Experimental
music by Nishinihon
words by Tsuyama Atsushi
recorded live at Tokuzo (Nagoya), 19th March 1999
produced by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Buddha... 佛陀...
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the second album of Uchū that is the first side unit of Acid Mothers Temple's Soul Collective.
The band cut-up their lyrics. They got this idea from Kawabata's experience to play "improvised radio drama" on his friend's unlicensed personal FM station when he was 17 years old.
The track 4 "Shamusun" is dedicated to Shamusun (acted by Fubuki Jun) on episode 215 of "Tokuso Saizensen (特捜最前線)".
The original CDR was released by Acid Mothers Temple label (limited 100 copies) in 1999 and reissued on CD as 2 in 1 with their 1st album "Uchū." by Namu in 2006.
This digital album was remastered by Kawabata Makoto for Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released February 5, 2021

2. Hitoribocchi Ichiman Konen ひとりぼっち一万光年

Kawabata Makoto : guitar, voice
Ayano : voice
music by Kawabata Makoto and Higashi Hiroshi

recorded at Studio Irori, 25th Feb. 1999
engineered by Higashi Hiroshi
mixed by Kawabata Makoto and Higashi Hiroshi
remastered by Kawabata Makoto, 2021
art work by Kawabata Sachiko
"No synthesizer, No Sampler, N programing... Only guitars!!"
The book of Universal Revelations is finally uncovered in 1999, the last year of the millennium!

Kawabata Makoto.title: Cosmic Pigs vs Invisible Ameba Men 宇宙豚 vs 透明アメーバ人間
Kawabata Makoto.about:
"Cosmic Pigs vs Invisible Ameba Men" was recorded in 1979 and 1980 at Horyuji Sound House and R.E.P. Studio (i.e. in Kawabata's room). At the time everyone in the group was in thrall to progressive rock, electronic music and musique concrete. Those obsessions led us to create a science-fiction story called "Cosmic Pigs vs Invisible Ameba Men" that within used as a theme for improvisation and effects manipulation. We thought we had come with a progressive rock masterpiece, but the only reaction from those who heard it was 'I don't get it'.
The story goes as follows. The Cosmic Pigs, who live on Planet Pigsty, awaken to a sense of self and they start a rebellion against their rulers, the Invisible Ameba Men. The rebellion is crushed by the appearance of the Giant Ameba deity, and its leaders are sent to the guillotine. The remaining Cosmic Pigs are enslaved, but thanks to the magical intercession of their savior, the Lord of the Cosmic Pigs, they acquire Diamond Eyes. The Invisible Ameba Men want the diamond eyes for themselves, so they slaughter the Cosmic Pigs until they are wiped out. However, once the Invisible Ameba Men have the diamond eyes, they lose the ability to become invisible and they in turn are attacked and exterminated by a new invader, the Mitochondrian.
The original release of this material was as a 90 minute cassette tape by R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1980, then re-issued on a 12 CDR box set entitled “Kawabata Makoto Early Works 1978-1983 : Learning From The Past - R.E.P. Reissue Series Vol.2” in 2012.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released April 21, 2021

Kawabata Makoto.title: Cosmic Rainbow Love
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is an unreleased live recording of Pikacyu-Makoto’s “acoustic set” in 2014.
All music and words (except for “Birth Star”) were totally improvised.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
Pikacyu-Makoto :
Formed love and peace cosmic duo, consisting of PIKA from Osaka primitive wild girls duo Afrirampo and Makoto Kawabata from psychedelic incarnations Acid Mothers Temple: male and female, angel and devil, yang and yin.
PIKA brings half-shrine maiden/half-wild vocals, drums and mystifying performances. Kawabata's guitar-work moves from a roar to a whisper, a yell to a sob. Together they create truly outer cosmic hymns to love and inner cosmic odes: a PIKAMAKO Word that expands into the limitless reaches of space!
The duo was originally formed in 2007 and have played extensively in Japan. In 2011 they spent five weeks touring the US and their first album, "OM Sweet Home : We Are Shining Stars From Darkside" was released by the UK label Riot Season. Their second album "Galaxilympics" was released by the UK label Upset! The Rhythm, and toured Europe to celebrate in 2017. In 2020 they should have played at "FIMAV (Festival International de Musique Actuelle de Victoriaville)", it was cancelled by covid19.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released October 21, 2021
1. End Of Egypt エジプトの果て
2. Burning The Earth バーニング・アース
3. Osaka Egypt Here And Now 大阪・エジプト・ヒア・アンド・ナウ
4. Memory Of Water 水の記憶
5. Birth Star バース・スター
PIKA : voice, percussion
MAKOTO : acoustic guitar, electronics, voice
all words & music were totally improvised. (excerpt for “Birth Star”)
recorded live at Kanadian (Osaka) 5th July 2014
recorded by Takayama Manabu
produced and mastered by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Double Decade or Diend
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Acid Mothers Guru Guru's 20th anniversary album.
Recorded live in Kyoto in 2024 with some studio overdubs.
Includes semi-self-covers of early Guru Guru songs.
Guru Guru is still active in Germany, but Acid Mothers Guru Guru's deep psychedelic sound is reminiscent of the early Guru Guru, which led Mani Neumeier to say, "This is the real Guru Guru" when Acid Mothers Guru Guru was formed.
Simultaneous release of digital album and limited CD on Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released March 6, 2026

Kawabata Makoto.title: Electric Guitars
Kawabata Makoto.artist: Kawabata Makoto & Miyamoto Naoaki 河端一 & 宮本尚晃
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Massive guitar duo from Kawabata Makoto and Miyamoto Naoaki (aka.MNS), who should have been garnering a lot of attention as a noise guitarist for the new century.
Both guitarists plays full of feedback beautifully.
Miyamoto doesn't use any effect pedals, he plugged directly in his Fender amp that was put on just opposite.
The original CDR was released by Acid Mothers Temple in 2001.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Electric Reincarnation
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Andromelos were formed back in 2004 by Yamazaki Maso (electronics) aka.Masonna, Kawabata Makoto (guitar, organ), and Okano Futoshi (drums). They released their first self-titled album on the Finnish label Ektro Records in 2006. But since then, for all kinds of tedious reasons, the band have been unable to play even a single gig.
But they were back as Andromelos2 in 2013, this time as the trio of Kawabata, Okano and Nakaya Koichi (electronics) from Nasca Car! They released their first album "Electric Reincarnation" from the French label BamBalam Records in November 2020.

Kawabata Makoto.title: 猟奇浪漫アイドルバンド エロチカ Erochika : Early Days 1984
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Erochika (1984 - 1992) is the X-certificate Japanese-style prog band that was led by Makoto Kawabata.
This original cassette “Erochika (猟奇浪漫アイドルバンド エロチカ) in a limited edition of 10, was sold at their last gig of this line-up (Kawabata formed with the new line-up in 1986) on September 1984.
This is the early recordings of Erochika, it’s really different from their later years even same songs.
Kawabata remastered for reissue on Bandcamp, and added an out take (different mix) and demo recordings for wanted members in 1985 as bonus tracks.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released October 14, 2021

- bonus track

Kawabata Makoto.title: えろちか Erochika : Kawabata's Demo 1986
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the demo tape of Kawabata’s new group Erochioka (mark 2), when he was looking for members.
Kawabata’s musical idea was Western rhythm + Oriental melody at that time. He inspired by Japanese traditional music, Indonesian Gemelan, Indian music and Arabic music.
When the female vocalist Kaoru joined in 1987, she wrote lyrics for some songs, and these titles were changed.
Kawabata plays all instruments, NO programing, NO sequencer.
This digital album was released only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Exuberance Unbalance
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is an unreleased live recording of GODMAN on "Message From Nonmalt Japan Tour 2018" in Osaka.
All music was totally improvised.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
GODMAN is:
Formed in 2005 by Kawabata Makoto (Acid Mothers Temple), Higashi Hiroshi, Miyashita Keiichi (Mandog), Enomoto Takayuki, and Watanabe Yasuyuki. Released their first album "GOD ∞ DOG" on the Acid Mothers Temple Label. Subsequently, the band shifted away from a fixed line-up, becoming instead an ad hoc, sporadically active unit led by Kawabata and Miyashita.
The band's latest line-up features minimal trance guitar, mixed with jazzy passages, of Miyashita Keiichi, aka Mandog. Miyashita is a true wandering guitar bohemian who has enjoyed a deep relationship with Damo Suzuki ever since he played as lead guitarist on the 2002 US tour by Damo Suzuki's Network. Joint lead guitar is provided by Kawabata "Guru" Makoto, leader and guitarist of Acid Mothers Temple who enjoy intense popularity outside Japan. Kawabata's guitar work ranges from the explosively loud and ultra-high speed to the ecstacies of eternal guitar drone. On transcendent bass is Morita Kiyoshi, who played in bands like Reform in the late 1970s and released a record on the legendary Pinakotecha label, before moving to Tokyo to become a studio musician. When he returned to Kansai he has been hailed by the younger members of the underground scene as a senior statesman. On drums is the unique, genius-level talent of Satoshima NANI. Satoshima, hero of Kansai's Generation Z (do we even need to keep saying that?), leader of Bogulta, and member of Acid Mothers Temple, Psyche Bugyo, Omoide Hatoba, Amazon Saliva, Human Shower and many more.
Together the sounds they play become the wind, the earth, shooting stars... They roam wild and free across the span of the cosmos, scorching onwards endlessly towards to the ends of the universe. Ultimate cosmic trance psychedelic music!
In 2018, from this line-up came the first new album from the group in 13 years, "Message from Nonmalt" and toured in Japan.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released October 7, 2022
1. Ikalus’ Fourth Dimension Room
2. Y-R Express
3. Chibull Commands

Kawabata Makoto : guitar, synthesizer
Morita Kiyoshi : bass
Satoshima Nani : drums
all music was totally improvised by GODMAN

produced by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Father Moo & The Black Sheep
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Cult trip document by Father Moo and his handmaidens.
Demonic scriptures of dubious eroticism and cultism, performed by the heretical guru Father Moo and his female disciples. Minimal acoustic works using just female chanting and drones.
The original CDR was released in a limited 100 numbered copies with a handmade gatefold sleeve by Acid Mothers Temple label in Aug.1998.
Swordfish Records reissued on LP in 2000, on CD in 2002.
Eclipse Records released the remix version on lathe cut LP in 2000.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Gekkyu Kekkaichi 月球結界地
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is Tsurubami's 5th album. It was released on CD from Strange Attractors Audio House in 2003.
Tsurubami was formed in 1994.
The three of us ? me, Higashi and a female drummer called Nobuko Emi are like three bloodbrothers whose destinies have been bound together from a previous existence. This bond between us later developed into the “Soul Collective” ideal of AMT.
The three of us have been given a huge mission in life and as a result we’re like waves that come and go, tides that ebb and flow. As long as we three have life on this planet the group will continue to exist. Here’s a summary of our mission:
According to the theory of Inyo Gogyo, everything that exists in the world can be expressed by Yin and Yang, the two principles. (even computers work in this way). Everything is created from Yin and Yang, and the interactions of the Five Elements - wood, fire, earth, metal, water. The workings of the universe can be understood through a knowledge of the Two Principles and Five Elements. The form of everything on Earth and in the heavens is created by Yin and Yang, while the workings of the world occur through the Five Elements. If we take it that sound is one particle of time, then the energy of sound flows from the deepest past into the far future. And that also suggests that the purpose of ritual and prayer is to recreate the energy that existed at the beginning of the cosmos. Rituals and prayer function to reawaken that which existed at the beginning, to open the doors of this world, and to make that energy flow once more. Through the holy sound of Ohm, can we glimpse the eternal, escape the constraints of time (past, present, and future), and come to meet the Buddha?
For us, Tsurubami is a place of eternal spiritual training, a place where everything must be laid bare. This band itself is the last and greatest stronghold of group existence. I see it as a place where we can experiment with a form of improvised playing based on paranormal communication which has existed since the time of the troubadours.
Tsurubami has taken a hiatus since the end of 2009.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released March 18, 2022

2. Seiitenrinengi　星位転輪縁起

Emi Nobuko : drums
Higashi Hiroshi : bass
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
All music was completely improvised and not overdubbed.
recorded at Acid Mothers Temple, 2002
produced, engineered and mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Golden Speed Messiah
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The original CDR was self-released as "Private Tapes.6" in Mar. 2002.
It's the compilation of Kawabata's unreleased solo works from mid 90's to early 00's.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released January 7, 2021
1. Cosmic Rooster
Kawabata Makoto : direct touching of a resister
recorded at Acid Mothers Temple, Aug. 2001
I came up with the idea for this track, consisting just of me touching a resister, when i appeared at Yamasaki Maso's (Masonna, Space Machine, etc.) "Cosmic Electronic Music Festival". The playing technique is as per the naming, with a little added echo. On this track i use a voyage Controlled Filter made by Oberheim Electronics Inc.
2. Vinash Odrha
Kawabata Makoto : voice, all instruments
recorded at Acid Mothers Temple, 1994
This track was recorded in 1994 for my as yet unreleased first solo album. So far only one another track from these sessions has been released "Bahai Darmha", which appeared on a free CD "Abus 43" that came with the French magazine Abus Dangereux in Feb.1998. And other tracks that were recorded in 1993 were released as a digital album "Yuga Hiku" on Bandcamp in Sep. 2020.
3. You Are All Of My Love
Kawabata Makoto : electric guitars
recorded at Acid Mothers Temple, Feb. 2002
I thought that i would try recording myself just sitting around the house, fingerpicking on the guitar, not playing anything in particular. If I was able to play just for the person I love most, how happy that would make me.
all music by Kawabata Makoto
produced, engineered and mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Guitar Soundcheck
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is one of the most unique Kawabata’s guitar solo album.
All tracks were recorded at soundcheck of Acid Mothers Temple European tour 2022.
Kawabata has had problems about the volume of his guitar amp against some sound engineers sometime.
He writes down “My guitar must be the loudest in the band, like three times louder than drums and bass. Please make the loudest in the universe!” on the tech-rider though, some sound engineers are afraid and ask him to make less volume. Then he answers “Please respect musicians.”
This digital live album was released for only Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Guru Guru Fest 2010
Kawabata Makoto.artist: Acid Mothers Guru Guru, Acid Mothers Temple SWR
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Live album featuring "Guru Guru Fest 2010".
Acid Mothers Guru Guru, a combination of Krautrock originator Mani Neumeire (Guru Guru) and Kawabata Makoto and Tsuyama Atsushi of Acid Mothers Temple, plays a deep psychedelic sound reminiscent of early Guru Guru.
The trio Acid Mothers Temple SWR, consisting of Tatsuya Yoshida (Ruins), Kawabata Makoto, and Tsuyama Atsushi, performs the most hardcore improvisation of all the units bearing the Acid Mothers Temple name.
The showcase tour, called "Guru Guru Fest", has been performed by Mani Neumeier, Tatsuya Yoshida, Kawabata, and Tsuyama, and is feature Acid Mothers Guru Guru, Acid Mothers Temple SWR, and Manitatsu (Mani + Yoshida). Acid Mothers Guru Guru, Acid Mothers Temple SWR, and Manitatsu (Mani + Yoshida).
The original CD was released by Chaotic Noise Recordings in 2010 includes these three units, but for this digital release, only two bands, Acid Mothers Guru Guru and Acid Mothers Temple SWR, are available.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released December 6, 2024

Kawabata Makoto.title: GUY UNIT Live 2014
Kawabata Makoto.about:
GUY UNIT is led by the guitar improviser Guy who is called "Emperor of Nagoya underground scene". He started playing electric guitar when he discover Surf music in mid 60's. Also he joined Derek Bailey's workshop, the he started to play improvised music. GUY Unit is his indeterminate improv unit, sometime trio, sometime big band. Kawabata Makoto joined GUY UNIT in 1997.
this line-up of GUY Unit is a improvised big band like Sun Ra Arkestra, but the concept is the antithesis of Free music, it's like Scam music. And GUY Unit play by mega-loud volume, the loudest improvised big band ever.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Hanshoh No Omoi 反照の想ひ
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is Tsurubami's 3rd album. It was released on limited CDR from Acid Mothers Temple label in 2001.
Tsurubami was formed in 1994. The three of us ? me, Higashi and a female drummer called Nobuko Emi are like three bloodbrothers whose destinies have been bound together from a previous existence. This bond between us later developed into the “Soul Collective” ideal of AMT.
The three of us have been given a huge mission in life and as a result we’re like waves that come and go, tides that ebb and flow. As long as we three have life on this planet the group will continue to exist. Here’s a summary of our mission:
According to the theory of Inyo Gogyo, everything that exists in the world can be expressed by Yin and Yang, the two principles. (even computers work in this way). Everything is created from Yin and Yang, and the interactions of the Five Elements - wood, fire, earth, metal, water. The workings of the universe can be understood through a knowledge of the Two Principles and Five Elements. The form of everything on Earth and in the heavens is created by Yin and Yang, while the workings of the world occur through the Five Elements. If we take it that sound is one particle of time, then the energy of sound flows from the deepest past into the far future. And that also suggests that the purpose of ritual and prayer is to recreate the energy that existed at the beginning of the cosmos. Rituals and prayer function to reawaken that which existed at the beginning, to open the doors of this world, and to make that energy flow once more. Through the holy sound of Ohm, can we glimpse the eternal, escape the constraints of time (past, present, and future), and come to meet the Buddha?
For us, Tsurubami is a place of eternal spiritual training, a place where everything must be laid bare. This band itself is the last and greatest stronghold of group existence. I see it as a place where we can experiment with a form of improvised playing based on paranormal communication which has existed since the time of the troubadours.
Tsurubami has taken a hiatus since the end of 2009.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Hot Rattlesnakes
Kawabata Makoto.artist: Kawabata Makoto & The Mothers of Invasion
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is one of my solo project Mothers of Invasion's 1st album.
We recorded it in London during the Acid Mothers Temple UK tour 2001.
This line-up (Tuyama on bass and Ichiraku on drums, both from Acid Mothers Temple at that time) were only this recording.
The Mothers of Invasion played gigs irregularly several times in Japan, but the line-up were changed each time.
Of course Mothers of Invasion is still active irregularly, and will record a new album.
The original cd was released by Paratactile in 2002, and reissued on CD by Prophase Music in 2006.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released June 17, 2020
1. Theme Of Hot Rattlesnakes
2. Fripian Flipped Over Niffy Their King Of Frippery
3. French Sweet Suger House
kawabata Makoto : all guitars
Tsuyama Atsushi : bass
Ichiraku Yoshimitsu : drums
recorded at The Moat studio (London) and Acid Mothers Temple (Nagoya) on May and August 2001
engineered by Toby Hrycek-Robinson and Kawabata Makoto
composed & produced by Kawabata Makoto
executive produced by Trevor Manwaring
art work by Kawabata Sachiko
special thanks to Acid Mothers Temple family, Glyn Collins, Stephen Robinson and Alan Cummings

Kawabata Makoto.title: 今はとて実落つるとは Imawatote Sane Otsurutowa
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is an unreleased live recording of Tsurubami in 2003.
It was a part of the showcase tour “Acid Mothers Temple Soul Collective Tour 2003”.
Tsurubami was formed in 1994.
The three of us ? me, Higashi and a female drummer called Nobuko Emi are like three bloodbrothers whose destinies have been bound together from a previous existence. This bond between us later developed into the “Soul Collective” ideal of AMT.
The three of us have been given a huge mission in life and as a result we’re like waves that come and go, tides that ebb and flow. As long as we three have life on this planet the group will continue to exist. Here’s a summary of our mission:
According to the theory of Inyo Gogyo, everything that exists in the world can be expressed by Yin and Yang, the two principles. (even computers work in this way). Everything is created from Yin and Yang, and the interactions of the Five Elements - wood, fire, earth, metal, water. The workings of the universe can be understood through a knowledge of the Two Principles and Five Elements. The form of everything on Earth and in the heavens is created by Yin and Yang, while the workings of the world occur through the Five Elements. If we take it that sound is one particle of time, then the energy of sound flows from the deepest past into the far future. And that also suggests that the purpose of ritual and prayer is to recreate the energy that existed at the beginning of the cosmos. Rituals and prayer function to reawaken that which existed at the beginning, to open the doors of this world, and to make that energy flow once more. Through the holy sound of Ohm, can we glimpse the eternal, escape the constraints of time (past, present, and future), and come to meet the Buddha?
For us, Tsurubami is a place of eternal spiritual training, a place where everything must be laid bare. This band itself is the last and greatest stronghold of group existence. I see it as a place where we can experiment with a form of improvised playing based on paranormal communication which has existed since the time of the troubadours.
Tsurubami has taken a hiatus since the end of 2009.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released March 3, 2022
1. 今はとて実落つるとは Imawatote Sane Otsurutowa
Tsurubami つるばみ:
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
Higashi Hiroshi : bass
Emi Nobuko : drums
music was totally improvised.
recorded live at Crocodile Cafe (Seattle) 17th June. 2003
produced & mastered by Kawabata Makoto

trackinfo.title: 今はとて実落つるとは Imawatote Sane Otsurutowa

Kawabata Makoto.title: "Immoral Dogma" Festival Overture For Tape, Synthesizer and Percussion (テープとシンセサイザーと打楽器のための祝典序曲「背徳のドグマ」)
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1982, then re-issued on a 12 CDR box set entitled “Kawabata Makoto Early Works 1978-1983 : Learning From The Past - R.E.P. Reissue Series Vol.2” in 2012.
This is one of my “wannabe contemporary composition” works in my teens, performed on synthesizer using mainly electronic tones. Having been shown the light by Stockhausen, I developed a yearning for contemporary classical music, and that impulse lay behind my creation of these solo pieces. Early on, from 1978, I had already created some solo electronic pieces for synthesizer, but those early pieces foregrounded musicality. In a more strictly rock-based approach. In these later pieces, however, the concept became more important as I groped for tunes and vibrations that could have a direct, drug-like effect on the brain and consciousness.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released December 23, 2021
1. Immoral Dogma pt.1 背徳のドグマ pt.1
2. Immoral Dogma pt.2 背徳のドグマ pt.2
Kawabata Makoto : tape, synthesizer, percussion
music by Kawabata Makoto
recorded at R.E.P. Studio, 1982
produced, engineered and mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Journey To Xanadu
Kawabata Makoto.artist: Kawabata Makoto + marron + Ishihara Takeshi 河端一 + marron + 石原岳
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The guitar trio by three musicians, Kawabata Makoto of Acid Mothers Temple, marron of Based On Kyoto, Ishihara Takeshi from Takae Okinawa. They have played as solo musicians in each different scene for a long time. Finally these 3 musicians could play together, and they tried to play as “improvisation by 3 solo musicians”, not as “improvisation by 3 guitarists”. Their music is totally mixture of their solo sets, not like bored guitar free improvisation.
This is unreleased live recording in Kobe in Spring 2022.Ishihara Takeshi synchronizes light bulbs with his guitar sound on the stage, also he makes noise by holding a cable in his mouth.
This digital live album was released for only Bandcamp first, also released on limited CD by Acid Mothers Temple label in 2022.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released June 12, 2022

3. Walk In The Electric Forest 電気の森を歩いて
4. Dancing On The Burning Titan Mountain 燃え盛るタイタン山の上で踊る私たち
5. Song Of Takae Town 沖縄県東村高江の唄
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
marron : guitar
Ishihara Takeshi : guitar, voice,
music by Kawabata Makoto + marron + Ishihara Takeshi
words by Ishihara Takeshi
(except for track-5 : music and words by Nanao Tavito)
recorded live at Helluva Lounge (Kobe) 30th April 2022
mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Kaina  腕
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The original CDR was released by Last Visible Dog on 2000.
It was reissued with the new art works and a bonus track on CD by Last Visible Dog on 2003
Including 2 studio recorded tracks and 1 live recorded track. (#3
When Tsurubami played this live track #2 "Kujiru 抉る" at the show, the timetable was so delayed by some support bands. As a result we had only 10 min.! Then we played only this song and Hiroshi ended the show with smashing his bass! (You can see the photo on tray of the CD.) That is, #2 "Kujiru 抉る" was the whole set of that night.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released October 30, 2020
1. Hitsumyou O Gotoshite 畢命を期として
2. Kujiru 抉る
3. Sekiei Sae Miezu 隻影さえ見えず

Higashi Hiroshi : bass
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
All music was completely improvised and not over-dubbed.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Kaiten, Love and Punk Rock (回天と愛とパンクロック)
Kawabata Makoto.about:
In 1980, at the time Kawabata Makoto and his friends / members of Baroque Bordello felt some sympathy with punk spirit but they just couldn’t get on with the music. So this was their own attempt at creating punk rock. The original release was a 46 minute cassette. With 32 tracks on the A-side more on the B-side - total of 62 tracks!
Unfortunately, virtually no one who heard our totally improvised take on punk understood what they were trying to do.
The original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1981, then re-issued on a 10 CDR box set entitled “Makoto Kawabata early works 1978-1981” in 2000. (Afterward, this 10 CDR box was changed to the 11 CDR + 1CD box.)

Kawabata Makoto.title: Kamikaze
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the previous Mainliner’s unreleased original 2nd album “Kamikaze”.
But this rejected album was self-released on cassette tape by Nanjo’s La Musica Records in 1997 just after the official 2nd album “ Psychedelic Polyhedron (1997)” was released from Fractal Records.
This album was also reissued as a part of “Mainliner La Musica Tapes” on limited CDR by Kawabata’s self-release in 2013.
This is the first recording with Yoshida Tatsuya who replaced Koizumi Hajime as the new drummer.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released January 8, 2022

Kawabata Makoto : guitar
Yoshida Tatsuya : drums
recorded at West Studio 1996

Kawabata Makoto.title: Kebaku Tenjite Myohasen 謦縛転じて冥播遷
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is Tsurubami’s studio live recording at KFJC in May 2003.
Tsurubami was formed in 1994.
The three of us ? me, Higashi and a female drummer called Nobuko Emi are like three bloodbrothers whose destinies have been bound together from a previous existence. This bond between us later developed into the “Soul Collective” ideal of AMT.
The three of us have been given a huge mission in life and as a result we’re like waves that come and go, tides that ebb and flow. As long as we three have life on this planet the group will continue to exist. Here’s a summary of our mission:
According to the theory of Inyo Gogyo, everything that exists in the world can be expressed by Yin and Yang, the two principles. (even computers work in this way). Everything is created from Yin and Yang, and the interactions of the Five Elements - wood, fire, earth, metal, water. The workings of the universe can be understood through a knowledge of the Two Principles and Five Elements. The form of everything on Earth and in the heavens is created by Yin and Yang, while the workings of the world occur through the Five Elements. If we take it that sound is one particle of time, then the energy of sound flows from the deepest past into the far future. And that also suggests that the purpose of ritual and prayer is to recreate the energy that existed at the beginning of the cosmos. Rituals and prayer function to reawaken that which existed at the beginning, to open the doors of this world, and to make that energy flow once more. Through the holy sound of Ohm, can we glimpse the eternal, escape the constraints of time (past, present, and future), and come to meet the Buddha?
For us, Tsurubami is a place of eternal spiritual training, a place where everything must be laid bare. This band itself is the last and greatest stronghold of group existence. I see it as a place where we can experiment with a form of improvised playing based on paranormal communication which has existed since the time of the troubadours.
Tsurubami has taken a hiatus since the end of 2009.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released September 14, 2021
1. Kebaku Tenjite Myohasen 謦縛転じて冥播遷
Tsurubami :
Emi Nobuko : drums
Higashi Hiroshi : bass
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
all music was completely improvised and not over-dubbed
recorded studio live at KFJC (Los Altos Hills CA), 19th May 2003

Kawabata Makoto.title: Kibo No Jubaku : Spellbound Hopes 希望の呪縛
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1981, then re-issued on a 10 CDR box set entitled “Makoto Kawabata early works 1978-1981” in 2000. (Afterward, this 10 CDR box was changed to the 11 CDR + 1CD box.)
Baroque Bordello broke up in 1983. This was the first release from our mid-period. From around this time, restarted to play gigs on a regular basis. We were aiming for a new kind of pop music, which christened "Kakusei Pop 覚醒ポップ [awakened pop]". The idea was to stimulate self-awareness through repetition.
However, at that time punk / new wave / noise bands were dominated the scene, and because we looked the same but were different, our music were not appreciated at that time.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released February 10, 2021
1. Kibo No Jubaku 希望の呪縛
2. Silent Prayer 黙祷
3. Good Bye Bordello #8 さらば第八娼館
Baroque Bordello :
Kawabata Makoto : g, b, syn, vo
Kawagishi Tetsushi : g, kb
Iwaki Yasuo : perc. syn. tapes
all music by Kawabata Makoto
recorded at Horyuji sound house, R.E.P. studio in 1981
produced, engineered and mixed by Kawabata Makoto
remastered by Kawabata Makoto in 2021

Kawabata Makoto.title: Lemonade Station Destruction 25
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Noise work based on Kawabata's sound source, overdubbed and edited by an American noisician Dental Work (Jay Watson).
"The music on this album is an official collaboration between Japanese Psychedelic Rock heavyweights Acid Mothers Temple frontman Kawabata Makoto and American Harsh Noise surrealist comedian Jay Watson of Dental Work, who together produce festering blisters and wounds that fizz volcanic sludge deep into your third eye, allowing a full vacation into mental paradise. This is the 3rd edition of this release, fully remastered with all new original artwork. The 1st and 2nd edition are now long out of print collectors items, the first edition selling for over $500 on e-bay for it's controversial, rare, banned album cover. Get it while you can, a must have for all fans of Acid Mothers Temple, Dental Work, and Placenta Recordings!"
[from Placenta Recordings official Bandcamp page]
placentarecordings.bandcamp.com/album/kawabata-makoto-acid-mothers-temple-vs-dental-work-lemonade-station-destruction-25-placenta-recordings-210-2010

Kawabata Makoto.title: Live Adios Dios
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is a live recording of the classical hard rock trio Adios Dios in 2013.
Adios Dios was formed by the legendary drummer Okano Futoshi (ex.Subvert Blaze, ex.Ghost, Hijokaidan, The Silence, Acid Mothers Temple & The Cosmic Inferno, etc), the extreme bassist Morita Kiyoshi (ex. Reform, Godman, Kuroi Mori, etc) and Kawabata Makoto in 2010. Each of the members have careers of over forty years.
The group focusses on improvisation in a rock idiom and initial impulse, but their shower of tumultuous moves and noise sounds more like a demon invasion. Just as their name suggests, this trio has no fear, even of the gods.
this digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released August 14, 2021
1. Smuggler
2. We Wanna Growl
3. We Fool No One

Okano Futoshi : drums
Morita Kiyoshi : bass
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
Recorded live at Helluva Lounge (Kobe) 17th February 2013
Recorded by Takayama Manabu
Enormous respect to all of our rock heroes

Kawabata Makoto.title: Live at Chat-Noir
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the latest live recording of the sound and visual collaboration project Sky Sine with the master of OHP light show liquidbiupil in Yokohama February 2023.
liquidbiupil is one of the best well known OHP light artist in Japan. liquidbiupil has played with Acid Mothers Temple also Kawabata Makoto’s many projects as collaboration, finally Sky Shine was formed in 2014, they played many shows also released 1st dvd “Live at Smith Memorial Hall” in 2015.
Of corse this live recording is not including liquidbiupil’s OHP light show though, Kawabata played music with liquidbiupil as like live soundtrack. if there isn’t liquidbiupil’s light show, Kawabata’s music should be totally different, that’s why this credit should be Sky Shine, not Kawabata Makoto solo.
This was the first time Kawabata played only acoustic guitar for Sky Shine, also this was the first time liquidbiupil played OHP without inks.
This digital live album was released for only Bandcamp.

liquidbiupil : OHP light show

Kawabata Makoto.title: Live at Darkroom 1979
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai (Dark Revolutionary Collective)
about this album:
Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai is Kawabata's first group that was formed in 1978.
This is Kawabata's first live recordings in his carrier. (The audience were only 2 friends.)
Ankoku Kakumei Kyodotai is just an initial impulse even there wasn't instruments like guitar, bass or drums.
The original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1980, then re-issued on a 12 CDR box set entitled “Kawabata Makoto Early Works 1978-1983 : Learning From The Past - R.E.P. Reissue Series Vol.2” in 2012.
This digital album was remastered by Kawabata Makoto only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.artist: Human Shower ヒューマンシャワー
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The original CDR was self-released for “HumAn Shower vs Sukebeisu Western Japan Tour 2020” on February 2020.
The nonsense art unit Human Shower (Kawabata Makoto and Satoshima Nani) formed in 2008, had played as improvised duo with guitar, drums and nonsense performance for 12 years. In 2020, the band tried to change to “new phase”. They started to use synthesizer and sampler more than past. In 2021, they started “Human Shower 2nd chapter”, they have totally changed to “total media show with improvised playing” in present.
This live recording is the rare document of the transition from 1st chapter to 2nd chapter.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Live & Rare
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the Pikacyu-Makoto's live recording album.
The original CDR has been released by ourselves.
Originally this live album was the limited European tour CDR (except for "Yurushimasu" that is included in 2nd album "Galaxilympic" ), then plus one unreleased track as the official live album "Live & Rare".
Pikacyu-Makoto :
Formed love and peace cosmic duo, consisting of PIKA from Osaka primitive wild girls duo Afrirampo and Makoto Kawabata from psychedelic incarnations Acid Mothers Temple: male and female, angel and devil, yang and yin.
PIKA brings half-shrine maiden/half-wild vocals, drums and mystifying performances. Kawabata's guitar-work moves from a roar to a whisper, a yell to a sob. Together they create truly outer cosmic hymns to love and inner cosmic odes: a PIKAMAKO Word that expands into the limitless reaches of space!
The duo was originally formed in 2007 and have played extensively in Japan. In 2011 they spent five weeks touring the US and their first album, "OM Sweet Home : We Are Shining Stars From Darkside" was released by the UK label Riot Season. Their second album "Galaxilympics" was released by the UK label Upset! The Rhythm, and toured Europe to celebrate in 2017. In 2020 they should have played at "FIMAV (Festival International de Musique Actuelle de Victoriaville)", it was cancelled by covid19.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:

Fujikake Masataka is a drummer who has played in various bands and sessions, including Zeni Geva, Akira Sakata x Triple Edge and Shibusashirazu Orchestra.
Based on a live recording made in January 2023 at Knuttel House in Asakusa Tokyo, a a sanctuary of improvised music, the album was additionally recorded at Kawabata's home studio Acid Mothers Temple, and was completed.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Lost Broken Seal
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The Seventh Seal were one of Nanjo Asahito’s projects in the 1990s. The Seventh Seal were derived from Okami No Jikan when Itakura Mineko (Angel'in Heavy Syrup) joined the band.
Kawabata composed these songs for this new line-up though, Nanjo decided to separate from Okami No Jikan and named The Seventh Seal when their only album “The Seventh Seal“ was released.
This unreleased live recording is from their only one show under the name of Okami No Jikan yet in 1995.
The original source is a VHS tape that has some damages, Kawabata mastered for releasing on Bandcamp in 2022.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Makoto Mango
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the first collaboration of Acid Mothers Temple and Gong. Guru & Zero is the trio by Kawabata Makoto and Cotton (both from Acid Mothers Temple) and The founder member of Gong Daevid Allen. They toured West-coast US in 2002, and recorded this album in Australia in February 2003 and headlined at "Festival Les Trionales" in Paris 2003.
Kawabata and Cotton joined the Daevid's new Gong "you'N'gong" in 2002, then band changed the name to "Gong" and released a new album "Acid Motherhood (released in 2004)". But this band broke up, then Kawabata and Daevid formed a new group "Acid Mothers Gong" with Cotton and Josh Pollock (both from you'N'gong), Tsuyama Atsushi and Higashi Hiroshi (both from Acid Mothers Temple) and Yoshida Tatsuya (from Ruins) in October 2003.
The original CD was released by Swordfish Records in 2004.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released May 7, 2021
1. Kawabata Biodynamique
Guru & Zero :
Kawabata Makoto aka Makoto Mango : guitars & organic loopage
Cotton Casino aka Mango Cottongo : synthesiza & bio illogical yodelling
Daevid Allen aka Psycho De Lick : glissando guitar & songsmithery & arrangement
recorded on a humble 4 track cassette at the the Bananamoon Observatory, New Brighton, NSW, Australia.
produced & mixed by Daevid Allen at Crystalgrid Studios, Byron Bay.
engineered by Tony Wandela
cover drawing by Dingo Virgin

trackinfo.credits: from Makoto Mango, released May 7, 2021

Kawabata Makoto.title: Mars
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the 3rd album of an electric guitar duo by Jean-François Pauvros and Kawabata Makoto.
We played totally improvised with 6 amps (2 x Marshall JCM-900 + speaker cabinet, 2 x Fender Twin Reverb, 2 x bass amp), the dynamic range was micro like almost silence to macro like wall of noise.
When we headlined a festival in France, we were the loudest in all artists even there were many heavy metal bands!
We called our music "guitar symphony", coz we've tried to play like a symphony by only 2 electric guitars.
When we met first time at the recording studio in Paris in November 1999, we surprised and sympathized each others. Because We saw other bows! We didn't know we played "bowed guitar" each others before. Since then we have played many shows in Europe and Japan. Unfortunately our latest shows in May 2020 were cancelled by covid19.
The original CD was released by Prele in 2005. The 12 pages photo booked was included. (all photos by JF and Kawabata.)

Kawabata Makoto.title: Message From Cosmic Church
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The alchemical fusion of sound and light! Sky Shine is a collaborative unit consisting of Kawabata Makoto, founder of the school of ecstatic guitar drone and lighting magicians liquidbiupil. Together they trace mandalas of sound and light, making manifest weird psychedelic spaces. Union with the brahman, opening the gate ti eternity!
The complete live recording at Smith Memorial Hall in Hikone, an unusual wooden building, originally a church and built by same specialist carpenters who works on temples and shrines. The interior of the hall fuses east and west and the lightshow transforms it into a trippy, dreamlike space, while the music resonates with the structure of the building itself. Genuine magic through light and sounds!
The originally the DVD has been released by AMT label in 2017.
Kawabata Makoto plays electric guitar and synthesizers. All music is improvised except for a riff of "Lost Milky Way".
Soundtrack to Kawabata Makoto's fictional fantastical space epic.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released May 30, 2024
1. In A Faraway Universe Where No One Knows
2. See You In The Moon Forest
3. Apostle From Cosmic Church
4. Lost Milky Way
Kawabata Makoto : guitar, synthesizer
liquidbiupil : OHP light show

recorded live at Smith Memorial Hall (Hikone), 10th December 2016
cinematography by aimaiworks and Takayama Manabu

Kawabata Makoto.title: Message From The Nonmalt (ノンマルトからのメッセージ)
Kawabata Makoto.about:
GODMAN is:
Formed in 2005 by Kawabata Makoto (Acid Mothers Temple), Higashi Hiroshi, Miyashita Keiichi (Mandog), Enomoto Takayuki, and Watanabe Yasuyuki. Released their first album "GOD ∞ DOG" on the Acid Mothers Temple Label. Subsequently, the band shifted away from a fixed line-up, becoming instead an ad hoc, sporadically active unit led by Kawabata and Miyashita.
The band's latest line-up features minimal trance guitar, mixed with jazzy passages, of Miyashita Keiichi, aka Mandog. Miyashita is a true wandering guitar bohemian who has enjoyed a deep relationship with Damo Suzuki ever since he played as lead guitarist on the 2002 US tour by Damo Suzuki's Network. Joint lead guitar is provided by Kawabata "Guru" Makoto, leader and guitarist of Acid Mothers Temple who enjoy intense popularity outside Japan. Kawabata's guitar work ranges from the explosively loud and ultra-high speed to the ecstacies of eternal guitar drone. On transcendent bass is Morita Kiyoshi, who played in bands like Reform in the late 1970s and released a record on the legendary Pinakotecha label, before moving to Tokyo to become a studio musician. When he returned to Kansai he has been hailed by the younger members of the underground scene as a senior statesman. On drums is the unique, genius-level talent of Satoshima NANI. Satoshima, hero of Kansai's Generation Z (do we even need to keep saying that?), leader of Bogulta, and member of Acid Mothers Temple, Psyche Bugyo, Omoide Hatoba, Amazon Saliva, Human Shower and many more.
Together the sounds they play become the wind, the earth, shooting stars... They roam wild and free across the span of the cosmos, scorching onwards endlessly towards to the ends of the universe. Ultimate cosmic trance psychedelic music!
In 2018, from this line-up came the first new album from the group in 13 years, "Message from Nonmalt"!
The original CD was released by Acid Mothers Temple label in 2018.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released December 17, 2020
1. Goldova (ゴルドヴァ)
2. Iyamounne (イヤモン)

Kawabata Makoto.title: Midsummer Pilgrimage In Systema Solare
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is an unreleased live recording of the sound and visual collaboration project Sky Sine with the master of OHP light show liquidbiupil at Daianzenji Temple Fukui in 2014.
liquidbiupil is one of the best well known OHP light artist in Japan. liquidbiupil has played with Acid Mothers Temple also Kawabata Makoto’s many projects as collaboration, finally Sky Shine was formed in 2014, they played many shows also released 1st dvd “Live at Smith Memorial Hall” in 2015. Of corse this live recording is not including liquidbiupil’s OHP light show though, Kawabata played music with liquidbiupil as like live soundtrack. if there isn’t liquidbiupil’s light show, Kawabata’s music should be totally different, that’s why this credit should be Sky Shine, not Kawabata Makoto solo.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: MURA
Kawabata Makoto.about:
MURA was an experimental rock band formed by Kawabata Makoto with some high school friends who had never involved with music or performance before. Their complete and utter musical amateurism allowed for a true flowering of the original creative impulse. Once that creative impulse began to ebb MURA split up, leaving behind four cassette and a single live performance.
Since the performance were originally recorded on to 30 minute cassette tape.(except the 4th, which used a 46 min. cassette for some reason.)
The original cassette tape was released only a few copie by Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1981, then re-issued on a 12 CDR box set entitled “Kawabata Makoto Early Works 1978-1983 : Learning From The Past - R.E.P. Reissue Series Vol.2” in 2012.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:

Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the latest live recording of the sound and visual collaboration project Sky Sine with the master of OHP light show liquidbiupil in Yokohama August 2022.
liquidbiupil is one of the best well known OHP light artist in Japan. liquidbiupil has played with Acid Mothers Temple also Kawabata Makoto’s many projects as collaboration, finally Sky Shine was formed in 2014, they played many shows also released 1st dvd “Live at Smith Memorial Hall” in 2015.
Of corse this live recording is not including liquidbiupil’s OHP light show though, Kawabata played music with liquidbiupil as like live soundtrack. if there isn’t liquidbiupil’s light show, Kawabata’s music should be totally different, that’s why this credit should be Sky Shine, not Kawabata Makoto solo.
This was the first time Kawabata played acoustic guitar for Sky Shine, he plays electric & acoustic guitars and synthesizers in this time.
This digital live album was released for only Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Not One Star Will Stand The Night
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Rebel Powers is the special unit by David Keenan and early Acid Mothers Temple.
This album was recorded in London 1998, when AMT stayed in London for their first tour.
The original CD was released by Strange Attractors Audio House in 2003.
The Night" Kawabata Makoto (ACID MOTHERS TEMPLE), Cotton Casino(ACID MOTHERS TEMPLE) & original AMT drummer Koizumi Hajime met up w/ David Keenan , guitarist for Glasgow's Telstar Ponies & prominent scribe for The Wire mag, & holed up in London's Moat Studios to let the heavenly river flow. Rebel Powers explore minimalist sound layering via 2 long, improvised tracks. Pools of sound shimmer & meticulously waver as each note strikes its placid surface, building and building an ominous mood w/ glacial intensity towards the psyche's breaking point...
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released March 17, 2021
1. We Are For The Dark
2. Our God Is A Mighty Fortress

David Keenan : guitar
Kawabata Makoto : guitar, sarangi
Cotton Casino : voice
Koizumi Hajime : percussion
music by Rebel Powers
recorded at Moat Studio (London) in 28th June 1998
engineered by Toby Hrycek-Robinson
mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Not "Very Very Famous Songs" vol.1
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Zoffy:
Formed by Tsuyama Atsushi (Psyche Bugyo, Omoide Hatoba, ex.Acid Mothers Temple & The Melting Paraiso U.F.O.) and Kawabata Makoto in 1998.
Zoffy’s music hints both at the members’ deep musical knowledge via their improvised performances of troubadour music and European trad, and also at their deep love for rock via their destructivist covers of classic rock songs, that no longer sounds like anything but ZOFFY. The group believes that humor is essential in music, and their unprecedented performances stray far from the beaten track. Is this the Ultima Thule that rock music has long been aiming for?
Zoffy is well known as playing innovative comical covers of famous songs, but they also play improvised fake trad. & folk music too.
This is the Zoffy's special best compilation vol.1 except for "Very Very Famous Songs" from their rare albums.
track 1 from "Atsumako - Very Best Of Zoffy"
track 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7 from 2nd album "Zo Zo Zo Zo Zoffy!!!"
track 8, 9, 10, 11, 12 from 3rd album "Thou Shalt Not Mess With Zoffy!!"
2nd album "Zo Zo Zo Zo Zoffy!!!" was released as a CDR in a limited edition of 100 copies as part of the Acid Mothers Temple label's "Gold Disc Series" in 1998.
Recorded at Acid Mothers Temple 4th - 5th Nov. 1998.
It was reissued on LP with a gatefold sleeve with new art work by Synesthetic Recordings in 2002.
The best compilation "Atsumako - Very Best Of Zoffy" was released as CDR in a limited edition of 100 copies as part of the Acid Mothers Temple label's "Gold Disc Series" in 1999. This album is including unreleased tracks.
3rd album "Thou Shalt Not Mess With Zoffy!!" was released on CD by Acid Mothers Temple label in 2003.
Recorded at Acid Mothers Temple Jun. - Aug. 2003

Kawabata Makoto.title: Nubatama  射干玉
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the unreleased demo recording of Tsurubami in 1994.
The original source is a cassette tape that is mixed at that time.
Tsurubami was formed in 1994.
The three of us ? me, Higashi and a female drummer called Nobuko Emi are like three bloodbrothers whose destinies have been bound together from a previous existence. This bond between us later developed into the “Soul Collective” ideal of AMT. The three of us have been given a huge mission in life and as a result we’re like waves that come and go, tides that ebb and flow.
As long as we three have life on this planet the group will continue to exist. Here’s a summary of our mission: According to the theory of Inyo Gogyo, everything that exists in the world can be expressed by Yin and Yang, the two principles. (even computers work in this way). Everything is created from Yin and Yang, and the interactions of the Five Elements - wood, fire, earth, metal, water. The workings of the universe can be understood through a knowledge of the Two Principles and Five Elements. The form of everything on Earth and in the heavens is created by Yin and Yang, while the workings of the world occur through the Five Elements. If we take it that sound is one particle of time, then the energy of sound flows from the deepest past into the far future. And that also suggests that the purpose of ritual and prayer is to recreate the energy that existed at the beginning of the cosmos. Rituals and prayer function to reawaken that which existed at the beginning, to open the doors of this world, and to make that energy flow once more. Through the holy sound of Ohm, can we glimpse the eternal, escape the constraints of time (past, present, and future), and come to meet the Buddha?
For us, Tsurubami is a place of eternal spiritual training, a place where everything must be laid bare. This band itself is the last and greatest stronghold of group existence. I see it as a place where we can experiment with a form of improvised playing based on paranormal communication which has existed since the time of the troubadours.
Tsurubami has taken a hiatus since the end of 2009.
This digital album was released only for Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released April 1, 2022
1. Nubatama 射干玉
2. Tenku No Yumi 天空の弓
3. Umizato 海座頭

Higashi Hiroshi : bass
Emi Nobuko : drums

recorded at Studio Soybean, 5th December 1994
produced, engineered and mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: O-Mandroid
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the first album by O-Mandroid, a super-generation improv / progressive post-punk band formed in the spring of 2022 by Kawabata Makoto, the Acid Mothers Temple drummer Satoshima Nani, and a great bassist hamachang from a Kobe based legendary post punk band BLONDnewHALF.
The LP released on Riot Season in the UK was edited for recording time, but this CD released on Acid Mothers Temple contains the original complete version before editing also different mastering by Nakaya Koichi from Nasca Car.
The embodiment of universal initial impulse, chaos swirling adult punk soul for adults!!
Vinyl LP & Digital album by Riot Season
riotseasonrecords.bandcamp.com/album/o-mandroid

Kawabata Makoto.title: OM Sweet Home : We Are Shining Stars From Darklside
Kawabata Makoto.about:
about this album:
This is the Pikacyu-Makoto's first studio recording album.
The original CD was released from Riot Season in 2011.
Pikacyu-Makoto :
Formed love and peace cosmic duo, consisting of PIKA from Osaka primitive wild girls duo Afrirampo and Makoto Kawabata from psychedelic incarnations Acid Mothers Temple: male and female, angel and devil, yang and yin.
PIKA brings half-shrine maiden/half-wild vocals, drums and mystifying performances. Kawabata's guitar-work moves from a roar to a whisper, a yell to a sob. Together they create truly outer cosmic hymns to love and inner cosmic odes: a PIKAMAKO Word that expands into the limitless reaches of space!
The duo was originally formed in 2007 and have played extensively in Japan. In 2011 they spent five weeks touring the US and their first album, "OM Sweet Home : We Are Shining Stars From Darkside" was released by the UK label Riot Season. Their second album "Galaxilympics" was released by the UK label Upset! The Rhythm, and toured Europe to celebrate in 2017. In 2020 they should have played at "FIMAV (Festival International de Musique Actuelle de Victoriaville)", it was cancelled by covid19.

Through the holy sound of Ohm, can we glimpse the eternal, escape the constraints of time (past, present, and future), and come to meet the Buddha?
For us, Tsurubami is a place of eternal spiritual training, a place where everything must be laid bare. This band itself is the last and greatest stronghold of group existence. I see it as a place where we can experiment with a form of improvised playing based on paranormal communication which has existed since the time of the troubadours.
Tsurubami has taken a hiatus since the end of 2009.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is the latest live recording of a duo of Kawabata Makoto and Acid Mothers Temple drummer Satoshima Nani.
Before joining Acid Mothers Temple, NANI was active as a duo called Human Shower with Kawabata, and is currently active as a duo with a different concept called Nani∞Guru, as well as with Triggered Sun, O-Mandroid, Erios Errors, Godman, and many others.

Kawabata Makoto.title: Outtakes as yet unreleased first album 没曲集
Kawabata Makoto.artist: Human Shower ヒューマンシャワー
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Human Shower is a zero-dimensional, zero-rythmical, ultra-art duo created in 2007 by Acid Mothers Temple leader Kawabata Makoto and genius, weirdnik drummer Satoshima Nani. Satoshima was a founder member of Zuinoshin, one of the leading bands in the new Generation Z movement that appeared in Osaka around the start of the 21st century. He went on to Bogulta, and also got invited to play with leading Japanese underground bands like Omoide Hatoba and Amazon Saliva.
In performance the group are notorious for their improvised song structures, as well as their sublime and entirely improvised lyrics. Their performances, as you might expect from their ultra-art conceptual approach, go beyond the understanding of most humans and climax with the construction on-stage of massive, meaningless art objets.
They have been working on their first album for eleven years now and it still needs some work. But in a typically bold move they have decided to release an "Outtakes" CDR from that as yet unreleased first album.
Human Shower has done their chapter one in 2019, and the band is touring the page to start chapter two from 2020.
Satoshima Nani has been the drummer with Acid Mothers Temple & The Melting Paraiso U.F.O. since 2015. Also Kawabata and Satoshima formed another duo with the different concept "NANI∞GURU" in February 2018.
This digital album is included all tracks from the original CDR and unreleased 4 bonus tracks.
And Kawabata remastered all tracks.
it was released only for Bandcamp.

Kawabata Makoto.title: PikaMako’s Lather Gather Together!! ピカマコの大興奮！全員集合！
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is an unreleased live recording of Pikachu-Makoto (Pika from Afrirampo + Kawabata Makoto) in London 2016.
This digital live album was released only for Bandcamp.
Pikacyu-Makoto :
Formed love and peace cosmic duo, consisting of PIKA from Osaka primitive wild girls duo Afrirampo and Makoto Kawabata from psychedelic incarnations Acid Mothers Temple: male and female, angel and devil, yang and yin.
PIKA brings half-shrine maiden/half-wild vocals, drums and mystifying performances. Kawabata's guitar-work moves from a roar to a whisper, a yell to a sob. Together they create truly outer cosmic hymns to love and inner cosmic odes: a PIKAMAKO Word that expands into the limitless reaches of space!
The duo was originally formed in 2007 and have played extensively in Japan. In 2011 they spent five weeks touring the US and their first album, "OM Sweet Home : We Are Shining Stars From Darkside" was released by the UK label Riot Season. Their second album "Galaxilympics" was released by the UK label Upset! The Rhythm, and toured Europe to celebrate in 2017. In 2020 they should have played at "FIMAV (Festival International de Musique Actuelle de Victoriaville)", it was cancelled by covid19.

Kawabata Makoto.title: “Pleasant Fetuses’ Land” Impromptu For Synthesizer (シンセサイザーの為の即興曲「愉快な胎児園」)
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This original cassette tape was released only a few copies on Kawabata's R.E.P. (Revolutionary Extrication Project) in 1982, then re-issued on a 12 CDR box set entitled “Kawabata Makoto Early Works 1978-1983 : Learning From The Past - R.E.P. Reissue Series Vol.2” in 2012.
This is one of my “wannabe contemporary composition” works in my teens, performed on synthesizer using mainly electronic tones. Having been shown the light by Stockhausen, I developed a yearning for contemporary classical music, and that impulse lay behind my creation of these solo pieces. Early on, from 1978, I had already created some solo electronic pieces for synthesizer, but those early pieces foregrounded musicality. In a more strictly rock-based approach. In these later pieces, however, the concept became more important as I groped for tunes and vibrations that could have a direct, drug-like effect on the brain and consciousness.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released November 19, 2021

Kawabata Makoto.title: Psychic Buddha Trip U.F.O. Death & High!!!
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is Kawabata’s private home recording of the sound rough sketch for the new project in 1993. This idea was realized as Acid Mothers Temple a few years later.
When he got a secondhand DJ mixer from a friend, he recorded some music collage for the basic track. The basic tracks were played reverse by a tape machine, then he edited and overdubbed guitar and electronics.
The original cassette tape was forgotten and lost for a long time. (Accidentally Kawabata found it in July 2021.)
This digital album was released for only Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released September 14, 2021

Kawabata Makoto.title: Reverse Fault  逆断層
Kawabata Makoto.about:
The original CD was released on Nov. 2022.
This beautiful and mysterious piece of sympathetic acoustic music transcends conventional concepts, pushing the possibilities of musical instruments to the limit, and at first listen it's hard to believe it's just drums and guitar.
Their unique sound, which is more reminiscent of "contemporary music" or "experimental music" than so-called "free music," is not only the result of a variety of unique playing techniques that they have devised themselves, but is also backed by the two musicians' wide-ranging musical background, experience, and knowledge.
It was precisely because of the COVID-19 pandemic that this piece was created by connecting Truth or Consequences New Mexico USA, and Asuka Village Nara Japan, via file exchange. Enjoy this slightly different sympathetic resonance from a regular improvisational performance.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released February 6, 2026

Kawabata Makoto.title: Shohjohkisshohtan 清浄吉祥譚
Kawabata Makoto.about:
This is Tsurubami's 6th album. It was released on CD from C3R Records in 2005.
Tsurubami was formed in 1994. The three of us ? me, Higashi and a female drummer called Nobuko Emi are like three bloodbrothers whose destinies have been bound together from a previous existence. This bond between us later developed into the “Soul Collective” ideal of AMT.
The three of us have been given a huge mission in life and as a result we’re like waves that come and go, tides that ebb and flow. As long as we three have life on this planet the group will continue to exist. Here’s a summary of our mission:
According to the theory of Inyo Gogyo, everything that exists in the world can be expressed by Yin and Yang, the two principles. (even computers work in this way). Everything is created from Yin and Yang, and the interactions of the Five Elements - wood, fire, earth, metal, water. The workings of the universe can be understood through a knowledge of the Two Principles and Five Elements. The form of everything on Earth and in the heavens is created by Yin and Yang, while the workings of the world occur through the Five Elements. If we take it that sound is one particle of time, then the energy of sound flows from the deepest past into the far future. And that also suggests that the purpose of ritual and prayer is to recreate the energy that existed at the beginning of the cosmos. Rituals and prayer function to reawaken that which existed at the beginning, to open the doors of this world, and to make that energy flow once more. Through the holy sound of Ohm, can we glimpse the eternal, escape the constraints of time (past, present, and future), and come to meet the Buddha?
For us, Tsurubami is a place of eternal spiritual training, a place where everything must be laid bare. This band itself is the last and greatest stronghold of group existence. I see it as a place where we can experiment with a form of improvised playing based on paranormal communication which has existed since the time of the troubadours.
Tsurubami has taken a hiatus since the end of 2009.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released February 27, 2021
1. Kandeki No Hate Ni 陥溺の果てに
2. Kimi O Omoite 君を想ひて
3. Shohjohkisshohtan 清浄吉祥譚
Tsurubami :
Emi Nobuko : drums
Higashi Hiroshi : bass
Kawabata Makoto : guitar
all music was completely improvised, not over-dubbed.
recorded at Acid Mothers Temple, Sep. 2004
produced, engineered and mixed by Kawabata Makoto

Kawabata Makoto.title: Sinking of Space Luxury Liner Gorgophone
Kawabata Makoto.about:
Andromelos were formed back in 2004 by Yamazaki Maso (electronics) aka.Masonna, Kawabata Makoto (guitar, organ), and Okano Futoshi (drums). They released their first self-titled album on the Finnish label Ektro Records in 2006. But since then, for all kinds of tedious reasons, the band have been unable to play even a single gig.
But they were back as Andromelos2 in 2013, this time as the trio of Kawabata, Okano and Nakaya Koichi (electronics) from Nasca Car. They released their first album "Electric Reincarnation" from the French label BamBalam Records in November 2020.
This is the live recording of Andromelos2’s memorable debut live in 2013.
This digital live album was released for only Bandcamp.
Kawabata Makoto.credits:
released September 3, 2021
